content.full_text:
October 2, 1979

Mrs. Rubell Helgeson
Pacific Palisades Property Owners
Association, Incorporated
Post Office Box 617
Pacific Palisades, California 90272

Dear Mrs. Helgeson:

Thank you for your letter of September 28, 1979 in which you
asked for verification of the lobbyist registration requirements
under the Political Reform Act.

In the letter you stated that members of the Pacific Palisades
Property Owners Association, Incorporated (Association) made
trips to Sacramento during the past year specifically to defeat
proposed legislation. Furthermore, none of the members of the
Association have received compensation for the time devoted to
this activity.

Government Code Section 82039 defines a lobbyist as "any person
who is employed or contracts for economic consideration, other than
reimbursement for reasonable travel expenses, to communicate
directly or through his agents with any elective state official,
agency official or legislative official for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action, if a substantial or
regular portion of the activities for which he receives consideration is for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative
action." (emphasis added).

A lobbyist is a person who is compensated for the purpose of
attempting to influence legislative or administrative action, and
who engages in such activity on a substantial or regular basis.
Therefore, members of the association are not required to register
as lobbyists because they have not received compensation for their
efforts to defeat legislation.

If I can be of further assistance, please contact me.

Sincerely,


Rich Eichman
Accounting Specialist

RE:bw

PACIFIC PALISADES PROPERTY OWNERS ASSOCIATION, INCORPORATED

POST OFFICE BOX 617
PACIFIC PALISADES,
CALIFORNIA 90272

September 26, 1979

Mr John Keplinger
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street, 3rd floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr Keplinger:

Les Cohen of Les S. Cohen and Associates has
supplied us with copies of his September 4, 1979
letter to you, challenging the propriety of our
activities in the State Capitol, which are not
registered under FPPC regulations.

It is our understanding that FPPC regulation
18239.e(2)(A), the time test quoted by Mr
Cohen, is a refinement of what constitutes
"a regular or substantial portion of the activities
for which (a lobbyist) receives consideration..." in
(82039). "Lobbyist" has already been defined in
this same section as "any person who is employed
or contracts for economic consideration..." to
influence legislation.

Members of our Board and our general membership
who have made trips to Sacramento during the
past year have done so to influence legislation,
specifically to seek the defeat of a bill designed
to benefit Mr Cohen's client; a bill we deem to
be meritless and of tremendous detriment to our
community.

However, none of us, including myself and Betty
Newman, have received any compensation whatever
for the time we have devoted to these activities.

We are not therefore "lobbyists" under the definition
of 82039, and are not subject to the subsequent
time test.

Since Mr Cohen's letter has had wide distribution,
we believe it is appropriate to ask you to verify
our standing under the appropriate FPPC rules, in
writing.

Many thanks for your attention to this matter.

Yours very truly,

Rubell Helgeson

cc: Les S. Cohen

OFFICERS
President
RUBELL HELGESON
Co-President
BARBARA KOHN
First Vice-President
BETTY NEWMAN
Second Vice-President
DONALD SEGEL
Corresponding Secretary
JAMES SOLOMON
Recording Secretary
JULIE HAAKER
Treasurer
ROBERT H. COLLINS

BOARD OF DIRECTORS
ANN ALPER
DOROTHY BISSELL
ROBERT CAVAGE
NEAL CROWLEY
ABRAHAM DWATT
MARGARET JOSE
CAROL LEACOCK
NANCY MARKEL
YOSHIKO RADTKE
MAXWELL SELMAN
REBA SOFFER
PETER STERN
NORMAN THROWER

CONSULTANTS
JOSEPH S. ANNINO
WARREN WILSON

DEDICATED to the preservation of the residential character of our community.

sections.question: Are members of the Pacific Palisades Property Owners Association, Incorporated required to register as lobbyists under the Political Reform Act when they have made trips to Sacramento to influence legislation but have not received compensation for their efforts?
sections.conclusion: A lobbyist is a person who is compensated for the purpose of attempting to influence legislative or administrative action, and who engages in such activity on a substantial or regular basis. Therefore, members of the Association are not required to register as lobbyists because they have not received compensation for their efforts to defeat legislation.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether unpaid members of a property owners association who travel to Sacramento to oppose proposed legislation must register as lobbyists under Government Code Section 82039.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Members of the Association are not required to register as lobbyists because the definition of lobbyist under Government Code Section 82039 requires compensation for influencing legislative action, and the members received no compensation for their activities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from Helgeson dated Sept 28/26, 1979) and the FPPC response letter (dated October 2, 1979 from Rich Eichman). The response clearly addresses the question and provides a definitive conclusion based on statutory definition. Confidence slightly reduced due to some ambiguity in document dating (Sept 26 vs Sept 28) in the source material.

QUESTION: Are members of the Pacific Palisades Property Owners Association, Incorporated required to register as lobbyists under the Political Reform Act when they have made trips to Sacramento to influence legislation but have not received compensation for their efforts?

CONCLUSION: A lobbyist is a person who is compensated for the purpose of attempting to influence legislative or administrative action, and who engages in such activity on a substantial or regular basis. Therefore, members of the Association are not required to register as lobbyists because they have not received compensation for their efforts to defeat legislation.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that members of the Pacific Palisades Property Owners Association are not required to register as lobbyists despite traveling to Sacramento to oppose legislation, because they received no compensation for these activities and the lobbyist definition requires economic consideration.
